verticals.name: Procurement — Maverick Spend
verticals.tagline: Same engine, a non-financial ontology — the platform thesis, proven.
verticals.use_case: Enterprises lose margin and invite collusion when staff buy off-contract or split orders to dodge approval limits. The same engine checks each requisition against budget and the Delegation-of-Authority matrix — a completely different ontology, zero engine code changed.
verticals.what_you_see: Below: open purchase requisitions. Submitting one that exceeds the remaining budget is routed to the MLRO approval queue — the exact same governance gate as an AML account freeze.

verticals.name: Insurance — Claims Fraud (SIU)
verticals.tagline: Staged-accident rings, same graph engine as AML — different nouns.
verticals.use_case: A single insurance claim looks legitimate in isolation — organized fraud only shows up in the links between claims. The engine traverses from a suspicious claim to its garage, claimant and policy, surfacing entities that recur across supposedly unrelated claims. The signal is shared-node density: one workshop at the centre of many 'accidents'.
verticals.what_you_see: Click an alert to investigate. Watch the engine light up a ring of claims all routed through one garage, then recommend referring the case to the Special Investigation Unit (SIU).

signals.label: Shared-garage ring
signals.why: Many ostensibly unrelated claims routed through one non-empanelled garage.

verticals.name: Lending — Early Warning Signals
verticals.tagline: Predict NPA stress before DPD — common-director contagion + EMI bounces.
verticals.use_case: Loans rarely go bad overnight; stress is visible months before an account is classified a Non-Performing Asset (NPA), but the signals sit scattered across systems. The engine traverses from a borrower to its loans, directors and repayment history to assemble a live risk picture — catching distress spreading through a shared-control network before it surfaces in the books.
verticals.what_you_see: Click an alert to investigate. Watch the engine cluster borrowers under one common director and flag EMI-bounce stress, then propose a margin call or a credit-rating downgrade.

signals.label: Common-director cluster
signals.why: Multiple borrowers governed by one director — correlated stress.

signals.label: EMI-bounce stress
signals.why: A borrower receiving many sub-threshold partial EMIs — distress.

verticals.name: Wealth — Suitability & Mis-selling
verticals.tagline: SEBI suitability breaches — the audit log IS the compliance artifact.
verticals.use_case: Advisers face incentives to push high-commission products onto clients they don't fit, exposing the firm to regulator action and restitution. The engine traverses from a client to their suitability profile, sales, products and adviser, comparing what was sold against the client's documented risk appetite — and flags advisers who breach repeatedly across a book.
verticals.what_you_see: Click an alert to investigate. Watch the engine surface an adviser who sold a high-risk product to many low-risk-profile clients, then propose flagging the mis-selling for compliance.

signals.label: Adviser mis-selling cluster
signals.why: One adviser sold a high-risk product to many low-risk-profile clients.

verticals.name: Corporate — UBO / Ownership
verticals.tagline: Unwind circular ownership to the true beneficial owner — pure OAG.
verticals.use_case: Regulators require knowing the real human Ultimate Beneficial Owner (UBO) behind a corporate customer, but ownership is deliberately obscured through layered holdings across jurisdictions. The engine traverses the company-owns-company graph through multiple layers to compute effective control and resolve the true UBO — here the signal is the shape of the ownership graph itself.
verticals.what_you_see: Click an alert to investigate. Watch the engine unwind a loop of companies that own each other (hiding the owner) and a cluster sharing one UBO, then propose escalating to Enhanced Due Diligence (KYC review).

signals.label: Circular ownership
signals.why: Holding companies own each other in a loop, obscuring the UBO.

signals.label: Shared-UBO cluster
signals.why: Many companies declare one common ultimate beneficial owner.
